name: Coffee Cake Muffins
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Light brown sugar, firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Grease a 12-muffin pan cups; set aside.
3. In a small bowl, mix together pecans, brown sugar, 1 tablespoon flour, cinnamon, nutmeg, and butter; set aside.
4. In a medium bowl, sift together 1 1/2 cups flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, and salt. Cut in shortening until well combined.
5. In a small bowl, blend egg with milk.
6. Add the egg mixture to the dry ingredients with half of the pecan mixture; stir until just blended.
7. Spoon batter into muffin cups; sprinkle with remaining pecan mixture.
8. Garnish each muffin with a pecan half.
9. Bake 15 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Remove muffins from pan and cool on a wire rack.
Mixing muffin ingredients requires a special, but easy-to-learn technique. The liquid ingredients (milk, eggs, etc.) are beaten together and then added all at once to the dry ingredients. Mixing is kept to a minimum—just enough to moisten the dry ingredients. The batter should be lumpy, not smooth. These muffins freeze very well.
